summary: Small code-quality/compile-fix PR touching MetroTRK, game-mode code, and repository compile flags. It added missing self-header includes, converted old-style empty parameter lists to explicit void in three function definitions, and suppressed an integer-overflow warning globally via compile_flags.txt. No PR body or review discussion was present in the slice, so the exact triggering build issue is inferred only from the diff.
what_changed: Added #include "targimpl.h" at the top of src/MetroTRK/targimpl.c before the existing MetroTRK include.
Changed TRKTargetStopped() to TRKTargetStopped(void) in src/MetroTRK/targimpl.c.
Changed TRKTargetStop() to TRKTargetStop(void) in src/MetroTRK/targimpl.c.
Added #include "gm_1601.h" in src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c alongside the existing static header include.
Changed gm_80164ABC() to gm_80164ABC(void) in src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c.
smart_moves: Included each C file's corresponding public header so the compiler can check function definitions against declarations instead of relying on disconnected prototypes.
Used explicit void parameter lists for no-argument C functions, avoiding old-style unspecified-argument declarations and likely satisfying stricter warning or static-analysis settings.
Kept the fix narrowly scoped: only three files changed, with seven added lines and three deleted lines.
decomp_lessons: For C source files with matching headers, include the matching header in the implementation file to catch prototype mismatches early.
Use func(void), not func(), for functions that take no arguments in C code.
Repository-wide warning suppressions such as -Wno-integer-overflow should be recorded because they may hide intentional decompilation overflow patterns or compiler-behavior dependencies.
Very small compile-cleanup PRs can unblock stricter tooling without changing decompiled logic or symbol names.
naming_conventions: MetroTRK functions retained the TRKTarget* prefix: TRKTargetStopped, TRKTargetSetStopped, TRKTargetStop, TRKTargetFlushCache.
Game-mode functions retained generated gm_ address-style names such as gm_80164ABC and gm_80164840.
The bitfield local name unlockable_character_bitfield was unchanged and remains descriptive for unlockable-character state.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly-matching tactic was shown beyond preserving function bodies while changing prototypes/includes.
Prototype cleanup was performed without altering logic, which is generally safe for matching when it does not affect generated code.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find other source files defining no-argument functions with empty parameter lists instead of void.
Find C implementation files that do not include their corresponding public header.
Audit why -Wno-integer-overflow was needed and whether the warning is from intentional decompilation overflow behavior.
